label: IND Applications for Clinical Investigations - Clinical Protocols
title: IND Applications for Clinical Investigations: Clinical Protocols | FDA
text: Clinical Protocol should be submitted for each planned clinical study or trial. An original IND application submission lacking a clinical protocol is considered incomplete. Protocols for subsequent studies not submitted with the original IND application can be submitted later as Protocol Amendments. The main components of a clinical protocol are described in Guidance for Industry, E6 Good Clinical Practice: Consolidated Guidance. For Phase 1 trials, early developmental protocols should specify in detail all elements critical to safety, including safety assessments, toxicity monitoring, stopping rules, dose adjustment rules, and adverse event recording and reporting.

sections.heading: Protocol requirement
sections.text: Clinical Protocol should be submitted for each planned clinical study or trial, and an original IND submission lacking a clinical protocol is considered incomplete.

sections.heading: Relationship to amendments
sections.text: Protocols for subsequent studies not submitted with the original IND application can be submitted later as Protocol Amendments.

sections.heading: Phase 1 emphasis
sections.text: For Phase 1 trials, protocols should detail safety-critical elements such as safety assessments, toxicity monitoring, stopping rules, dose adjustments, and adverse event recording and reporting.

citation_spans.quoted_text: Clinical Protocol should be submitted for each planned clinical study or trial.
